title: Brin Hesk'l
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Brin}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Brin Hesk'l |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/swlf20040715|text=Into the Woods}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Brin Hesk'l is a treasure hunter, a daring man who has explored everything from the sewers of Coruscant to abandoned Hutt strongholds to lost Sith temples.|Ryk Osentay, on ''Eye on Cularin''|}} '''Brin Hesk'l''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] adventurer and [[Treasure hunting/Legends|treasure hunter]] who lived during the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Hesk'l investigated the [[Cularin's disappearance|temporary disappearance]] of [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]] from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Circa [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], during the course of his investigation the adventurer was interviewed by the [[Journalist/Legends|journalist]] [[Ryk Osentay]] for the news show ''[[Eye on Cularin]]''. Two [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] after his meeting with Osentay, Hesk'l disappeared and his [[Starship/Legends|starship]] was found adrift in a [[Cularin comet cloud|comet cloud]] on the edge of the [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]] [[Star system/Legends|system]]. ==Biography== ===Treasure hunter extraordinaire=== {{Quote|Once, while on Coruscant, I was wandering through one of the museums—they call it the Children's Museum, and it chronicles the impact young people have had on our galaxy—and I just happened to spot a crack in the floor that looked a little suspicious. Well, to make a long story short, this crack was not only hand-made, but it contained the trigger mechanism to open a secret room that had been designed by the original builders over two hundred years before, where some fairly important documents were stored.|Brin Hesk'l, during his appearance on ''Eye on Cularin''|}} Brin Hesk'l was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who operated as an adventurer, [[Scout/Legends|explorer]] and treasure hunter during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. During the course of his career, Hesk'l visited many [[Planet/Legends|planets]] in search of treasure and visited places as varied as abandoned [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] strongholds, lost [[Sith temple/Legends|Sith temples]] and the [[Sewers of Coruscant|sewers]] of the world of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Hesk'l also once visited the [[Children's Museum]] on Coruscant, an institution dedicated to highlighting the impact of young people on [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]; while there he spotted a crack in the floor that on further inspection he found to contain a trigger mechanism to open a secret chamber made by the original builders of the [[Museum/Legends|museum]]. Hesk'l then entered the chamber and uncovered some important documents concealed within. To record his exploits, in secret Hesk'l began work on writing a [[Book/Legends|book]] that he called the [[Codex (book)|''Codex'']], in which he recorded details of the places that he had visited and the objects that he had found during his adventures as well as his thoughts on the significance of his discoveries. ===Investigating the Darkstaff=== {{Quote|So I started reading up on this darkstaff thing. A real, live Sith artifact. It's been living here for how long? And I'll tell you something—items that powerful, they don't get found in just ones.|Brin Hesk'l, during his appearance on ''Eye on Cularin''|}} Around [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]],The ''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'' states that the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|Living Force]] campaign is set one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], an event that is dated to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. As the ''Living Force'' adventure ''[[Below the Belt: Philology|Philology]]'' depicts [[Cularin's disappearance]] and confirms that the [[Star system/Legends|system]] vanished from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for ten years, in can be concluded that [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]] disappeared in around [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]] and reappeared in around [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]]. The Living Force adventure ''[[Night Eyes: Night's Homecoming|Night's Homecoming]]'' also establishes that by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the events of the [[Night Eyes trilogy]] of the campaign, over a year had elapsed since the events of ''Philology''. ''[[Into the Woods]]'' and Hesk'l's trip to the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]] can therefore be dated to circa [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]]. the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]], a [[Star system/Legends|star system]] in the [[Thaere sector/Legends|Thaere sector]], [[Cularin's disappearance|vanished]] for ten years due to the [[Time travel/Legends|temporal effects]] of the [[Darkstaff]], a powerful [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]]. The cataclysm caught Hesk'l's attention and after the [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]] system returned to the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] in around [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the adventurer began researching the cause of Cularin's disappearance. From examining some restricted archives to which he should not have had access, Hesk'l learned of existence of the Darkstaff and the role that it played in had Cularin's temporary absence. Based on his knowledge of a tenet of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], an order of [[Monk/Legends|monastic]] warriors allied with the Galactic Republic{{CSWECite|II|150|Jedi Order}}, that the Force must be in balance, Hesk'l posited that as the Darkstaff was strong in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], there must be an artifact strong in the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]] present in the Cularin system that was acting as a counterbalance to the Darkstaff. Hesk'l's theory was correct; a powerful [[Light side artifact|light side-imbued artifact]] known as the [[Eye of the Sun]] was concealed in the system and was counteracting the Darkstaff's dark side [[Force power/Legends|power]].{{LivingForce|EndTimes: The Eye}} Determined to find the theorized light side artifact and keen to make a profit, around [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]] Hesk'l ventured to the Cularin system to search for the relic. Following his arrival on Cularin, the second planet in the system,''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'' Hesk'l was invited to be interviewed for [[Eye on Cularin]], a local news show recorded by [[Cularin Central Broadcasting]]. During his appearance on the program, Hesk'l answered questions from the [[Journalist/Legends|journalist]] [[Ryk Osentay]] about his career and on why he had come to the Cularin system, and Hesk'l explained his theory regarding the hypothetical counterbalance to the Darkstaff. Hesk'l also stated his intention to commence his search by venturing to [[Thaere Privo|Thaere]], the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of a [[Thaereian military|military force]] charged with maintaining security in the Cularin system.{{Wizards|url=rpga/livingforce/article.asp?x=lf/swlf20011004b_thaere&c=swlfc|text=Over Thaere}} Two [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] after the interview, Hesk'l's [[Starship/Legends|starship]] was found abandoned adrift in a [[Cularin comet cloud|comet cloud]] on the edge of the Cularin system, with his whereabouts unknown. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I guess I should wish you luck.''"
"''Nah. Luck is for people who need it. The Force is my ally.|Ryk Osentay and Brin Hesk'l, on ''Eye on Cularin''|}} A brash and self-absorbed man, Hesk'l was a daring and successful treasure hunter who understood all the intricacies of his trade. He was motivated to pursue a career as a treasure hunter by the adrenaline rush that he gained from entering places that he should not have been able to access and uncovering objects considered to be lost. Hesk'l also had a deep love for history and was driven by a desire to learn more about why events had happened and how to prevent past mistakes from happening again. He believed that he had no no need for luck in his pursuit of treasure as he thought that the Force was with him. Hesk'l opined that everything created had an exact opposite{{CSWECite|II|36|Hesk'l, Brin}}, and therefore hypothesized the existence of a counterbalance to the Darkstaff. Though he considered Coruscant to be one of his favourite worlds because one could obtain anything that they desired in the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|metropolis]], he also thought Coruscant to be an uninteresting world to explore because it had been heavily surveyed. He enjoyed searching remote planets that had been rarely visited and considered that the best hauls of treasure could be found on unexplored worlds. However, he also observed that treasure could occasionally be found in plain sight in places where it would be least expected. He planned to venture to Thaere during his search for the theorized light side counterbalance to the Darkstaff despite the [[Thaereian]] [[Army/Legends|military]] being embroiled in a [[Thaereian War|war]] with the [[Cularin Militia]] paramilitary force.{{LivingForce|Belted In: Incursion}} He regarded the conflict merely as politics and believed that contentious political issues could always be worked around by meeting with the right people and asking the right questions. Hesk'l had a swarthy appearance, with short, straight [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. He had large [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and a large, crooked chin that looked as if it had been damaged from many [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] fights. During his interview with Ryk Osentay, Hesk'l bore a large smile throughout, revealing his shiny, white [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] and giving him the appearance of a [[Caarite]], a diminutive [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] known for their friendly demeanor. In addition to [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], Hesk'l could speak, read and [[Writing/Legends|write]] the [[Language/Legends|languages]] [[Rodese/Legends|Rodese]] and [[Twi'leki/Legends|Ryl]] and also understood [[Shyriiwook/Legends|Shyriiwook]]. He proficient in the use of [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]] and [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]]. ==Equipment== Hesk'l operated a starship. He was armed with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] [[Pistol/Legends|pistol]] and he carried an [[Backpack/Legends|exploration pack]], spelunking gear and three [[A99 aquata breather/Legends|aquata breathers]], compact devices that allows one to breath in [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] environments.''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' He was also equipped with a [[Blast helmet/Legends|blast helmet]] and a [[Blast vest/Legends|blast vest]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|ln your campaign, the heroes might happen upon Brin just as he has uncovered a particularly desirable artifact, or after he has finally made a connection between events that someone went to a great deal of trouble to obscure. With someone who has been as many places and seen as many things as Brin has, there's no end to the ways he can help advance existing plots or introduce new ones into your campaign.|A suggestion in ''Into the Woods'', by Morrie Mullins, on how Brin could be integrated into roleplaying adventures|}} Brin Hesk'l was created by [[Morrie Mullins]] and appeared in the [[Wizards.com]] article ''[[Into the Woods]]'', which was published on [[July 15]] [[2004]]. The article was a tie-in to the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|Living Force]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] campaign and depicted an interview with Hesk'l for the news show Eye on Cularin. He later received an entry in the [[2008]] compendium ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ''Into the Woods'' provided [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] statistics for Hesk'l and suggested that he could be inserted as a non-player character into roleplaying campaigns set in any publishing era. The article hinted that the adventurer could be introduced into a roleplaying adventure by having the player characters could run into him just as he has found a sought-after artifact. The article also suggested that players could encounter Hesk'l just as he had uncovered a link between some events that that another party had gone to great effort to conceal. ==Sources== *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/swlf20040715|text=Into the Woods}} {{1stm}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hesk'l, Brin}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Scouts]]
